mnemonics.title: POSDCORB Functions of Management
mnemonics.content:
Planning - Organizing - Staffing - Directing - Controlling - Reporting - Budgeting
Mnemonic: 'Please Officers Should Deliver Correct Reports By Deadlines' (first letters form POSDCORB)

mnemonics.title: Balanced Scorecard Perspectives
mnemonics.content:
Financial - Customer - Internal Business Processes - Learning & Growth
Mnemonic: 'Famous Cars Interest Little Geeks' (F-C-I-L-G)

mnemonics.title: Steps in Control Process
mnemonics.content:
1) Establish Standards 2) Measure Performance 3) Compare with Standards 4) Analyze Variances 5) Take Corrective Action
Mnemonic: 'Every Manager Compares And Corrects' (E-M-C-A-C)

mnemonics.title: Responsibility Centers
mnemonics.content:
Cost Center - Profit Center - Investment Center
Mnemonic: 'Cost Profits Invest' (C-P-I in ascending responsibility order)

mnemonics.title: Variance Analysis Types
mnemonics.content:
Material Variance, Labor Variance, Overhead Variance, Sales Variance
Mnemonic: 'My Little Orange Sings' (M-L-O-S)

mnemonics.title: Management Skills at Different Levels
mnemonics.content:
Technical (Bottom), Human (Middle), Conceptual (Top)
Mnemonic: 'Technical Humans Create' (THC - T=H=Bottom, H=Middle, C=Top)

acronyms.expansion: Planning, Organizing, Staffing, Directing, Controlling, Reporting, Budgeting
acronyms.usage: Seven essential functions of management that all managers perform
Goal-setting technique where managers and employees jointly set and evaluate objectives
Strategic performance measurement tool with four perspectives: Financial, Customer, Internal Processes, Learning & Growth
Financial metric: (Net Profit / Investment) × 100 to evaluate investment efficiency
Integrated software system connecting all business functions into unified database
Computer-based system helping managers analyze data for semi-structured decision-making
System processing data into information for managerial planning and control
Performance metric measuring value created beyond cost of capital
Technique analyzing relationship between costs, volume, and profit

visualTips.visualization: Draw a circular flow: Plan/Set Standards → Measure Actual → Compare & Analyze → Correct Action → Back to Planning. Think of it as a thermostat cycle - setting temperature (standard), checking thermometer (measure), adjusting heating (corrective action)
Pyramid diagram with Cost Center at base (lowest responsibility), Profit Center in middle, Investment Center at top (highest authority). Width decreases as responsibility increases.

Four quadrants like a car dashboard: Financial (speedometer), Customer (fuel gauge), Internal Processes (engine indicator), Learning & Growth (navigation). All must work together for the 'vehicle' to run.
Two columns: Favorable (F) on left (green = good, cost under budget), Unfavorable (U) on right (red = bad, cost over budget). Draw arrows showing direction of deviation from standard.

Triangle hierarchy: Top (CEO) - 10% Technical, 30% Human, 60% Conceptual | Middle (Managers) - 30% Technical, 40% Human, 30% Conceptual | Bottom (Supervisors) - 50% Technical, 40% Human, 10% Conceptual

analogies.analogy: Like a car's control system - steering (planning), brakes (controlling), dashboard (reporting), all working together to reach destination (goals) safely and efficiently.
Like a family monthly budget - you plan expenses, track actual spending, compare where you overspent/underspent, and adjust next month's plan accordingly.
Like a recipe - the recipe specifies exact amounts (standard cost), if you use more/less ingredients (variances), you analyze why and improve next time.
Like a university grading system - each department (responsibility center) is graded based on what they control (cost = assignments, profit = projects with budgets, investment = research labs)
Like a fitness goal - you set a target (run 5km), track progress weekly, reward achievement, adjust if needed. Success depends on personal commitment to the goal.
Like a GPS navigation - it doesn't drive for you (not automated), but analyzes routes, traffic, fuel to help you make better driving decisions (semi-structured problems).
